edges.evidence_quote: The Bonanno crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the " Five Families " that dominate organized crime activities in New York City within the criminal organization known as the American Mafia.
edges.context: Bonanno family is a constituent part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia in heroin trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: The family was known as the Maranzano crime family until its founder Salvatore Maranzano was murdered in 1931.
edges.context: Bonanno family is the successor to the Maranzano crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The clan's strongest ally was the leader of the Magaddino clan Stefano Magaddino
edges.context: Bonanno's Sicilian origins clan was allied with the Magaddino clan.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 1900s, the two clans feuded with Felice Buccellato, the boss of the Buccellato clan.
edges.context: Magaddino clan feuded with Buccellato clan.

edges.evidence_quote: After the murders of Stefano and Giuseppe Bonanno, their younger brother, Salvatore, took revenge by killing members of the Buccellatos.
edges.context: Bonanno family (through Salvatore Bonanno) attacked Buccellato clan in revenge.

edges.evidence_quote: violence broke out between a faction led by Giuseppe "Joe the Boss" Masseria and members of the Castellammareses over the theft of Masseria's bootleg liquor. This soon developed into a full out war known as the Castellammarese War.
edges.context: Castellammarese clan fought Masseria clan in the Castellammarese War.

edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were Buffalo family boss Stefano Magaddino
edges.context: Maranzano family allied with Buffalo family.

edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were ... Detroit family boss Gaspar Milazzo
edges.context: Maranzano family allied with Detroit family.

edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano's allies were ... Philadelphia family boss Salvatore Sabella
edges.context: Maranzano family allied with Philadelphia family.

edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano was also close to Joseph Profaci, future boss of the New York Profaci family.
edges.context: Maranzano family had a close relationship with Profaci family.

edges.evidence_quote: they established a secret alliance with the Bronx Reina family boss Gaetano Reina
edges.context: Maranzano family secretly allied with Reina family.

edges.evidence_quote: With the support of Buffalo crime family boss Stefano Magaddino, Bonanno also expanded into Canada.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Buffalo family to expand into Canada.

edges.evidence_quote: Joseph became a powerful member of the Commission, owing to his close relationship with fellow boss Joe Profaci. The bond between the two became even stronger when Joseph's son Salvatore "Bill" Bonanno married Profaci's niece Rosalie in 1956. The Bonanno-Profaci alliance deterred the other three families from trying to move in on their rackets.
edges.context: Bonanno family was allied with Profaci family through personal and marriage ties.

edges.evidence_quote: Joseph Bonanno was now threatened by an alliance of Tommy Lucchese and new boss Carlo Gambino
edges.context: Bonanno family was in conflict with Gambino family in the 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: Joseph Bonanno was now threatened by an alliance of Tommy Lucchese and new boss Carlo Gambino
edges.context: Bonanno family was in conflict with Lucchese family in the 1960s.

edges.context: Bonanno family was formerly allied with Rizzuto family per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Rizzuto crime family
edges.context: Bonanno family later became rivals with Rizzuto family per infobox.

edges.context: Bonanno family is allied with Colombo crime family per infobox.

edges.context: Bonanno family is allied with DeCavalcante crime family per infobox.

edges.context: Bonanno family is allied with Trafficante crime family per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia to import heroin.

edges.evidence_quote: Genovese crime family boss Frank Tieri began contacting Cosa Nostra leaders to build a consensus for Galante's murder
edges.context: Genovese family sought consensus to kill Galante, involving Bonanno family.

edges.evidence_quote: Massino turned to Colombo crime family boss Carmine Persico and Gambino boss Paul Castellano for advice; they told him to act immediately.
edges.context: Massino consulted Colombo boss for advice on killing rival capos.

edges.evidence_quote: Massino turned to Colombo crime family boss Carmine Persico and Gambino boss Paul Castellano for advice; they told him to act immediately.
edges.context: Massino consulted Gambino boss for advice on killing rival capos.

edges.evidence_quote: Gerlando Sciascia, a Sicilian-born capo linked to the Montreal Rizzuto crime family, arranged a meeting... They had four gunmen, including Vitale and Bonanno-affiliated Montreal boss Vito Rizzuto
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Rizzuto family in murder of three capos.

edges.evidence_quote: the Bonannos had regained their Commission seat with Gotti's help.
edges.context: Gambino boss John Gotti helped Bonanno family regain Commission seat.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino crime family from New York and the 'Ndrangheta Mafia from Calabria were also involved.
edges.context: Bonanno associate involved in drug trafficking operation with Gambino and 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: Bonanno/ Gambino associate Franco Lupoi were involved in a large cocaine, heroin and weapons trafficking operation
edges.context: Bonanno and Gambino families cooperated in drug and weapons trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: The indictment alleged the Bonannos and Genoveses operated a joint illegal gambling establishment in Lynbrook called the Gran Caffe.
edges.context: Bonanno and Genovese families jointly operated an illegal gambling establishment.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2020, the Italian police together with the FBI discovered a strong link between the Bonannos and the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family, in particular to Francesco Domingo.
edges.context: Bonanno family has a strong link with the Castellammare del Golfo Mafia family.

edges.evidence_quote: he and his brother Dino, also present, were protected by a multitude of bikers from the Crazy Pistons Motorcycle Club in Brooklyn, who, along with the Cammaranos, got into a confrontation with the other mobsters.
edges.context: Crazy Pistons Motorcycle Club protected Bonanno members during a confrontation.

edges.evidence_quote: federal prosecutors indicted Bushawn Shelton, allegedly a high-ranking member of the Bloods gang, with attempting to hire a hitman to kill Sylvester and Salvatore Zottola.
edges.context: A Bloods member was hired to arrange a hit on Bonanno associates.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2012, he and acting capo John "Johnny Joe" Spirito Jr., as well as 20 to 25 Bonanno mobsters stormed the Lucchese family -managed Coddington Club in the Bronx as a "show of force".
edges.context: Bonanno mobsters stormed a Lucchese-managed club as a show of force, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Members from the Bonnano as well as the Genovese families were arrested.
edges.context: Members of both families were arrested together in a racketeering case, but no direct relationship is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.
edges.context: Bonanno family had close ties and cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia to import heroin.

edges.evidence_quote: Armetta was charged with assaulting an individual on Halloween 2015 to gain entry into the Bonanno family, and he was released from prison on February 12, 2020. He attended the wake of former Lucchese family consigliere Frank Lastorino in November 2022.
edges.context: A Bonanno soldier attended the wake of a Lucchese consigliere, indicating social ties.

edges.evidence_quote: Frascone was involved in a large-scale illegal gambling operation along with Genovese soldier Victor Colletti.
edges.context: A Bonanno capo and a Genovese soldier jointly ran an illegal gambling operation.

edges.evidence_quote: He was accused of affiliating with members of the Bonanno and Gambino crime families, including playing bocce with Louis Vallario, a capo of the Gambino crime family.
edges.context: A Bonanno associate socialized with a Gambino capo, indicating ties between the families.

edges.evidence_quote: In May 2000, DiFiore and two members of the Gambino crime family, capo Salvatore Scala and soldier Charles Carneglia, were arrested and charged with extortion.
edges.context: Bonanno and Gambino members jointly extorted a business, showing cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: Calabrese helped mediate a dispute with Colombo family associate Francis Guerra and capo Anthony Russo over Bonanno associate Eugene Lombardo stealing Guerra's relatives' pizza recipe.
edges.context: A Bonanno capo mediated a dispute involving a Colombo associate and capo, indicating interaction.

edges.evidence_quote: DeFrancisci is a founding member of the Bath Avenue Crew, the infamous Bonanno farm team.
edges.context: The Bath Avenue Crew is a farm team for the Bonanno family.

edges.evidence_quote: in 2016, he was indicted with Gambino family acting Capo John "Johnny Boy" Ambrosio and Gambino family associate John Saladino on loan sharking and racketeering charges.
edges.context: A Bonanno family member was indicted alongside Gambino family members, indicating cooperation in criminal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: It later became common knowledge that Stagno was shot by Terrance Caldwell, an associate of the Lucchese family.
edges.context: A Bonanno soldier was shot by an associate of the Lucchese family, indicating conflict between the families.

edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia to import heroin into the US.

edges.evidence_quote: as a favour to Gambino family boss John Gotti
edges.context: Bonanno affiliate Thomas Pitera committed a murder as a favor to Gambino boss John Gotti.

edges.evidence_quote: Asaro was acquitted of alleged involvement in the 1978 Lufthansa robbery with Lucchese family associates
edges.context: Bonanno member Vincent Asaro was involved in the Lufthansa robbery with Lucchese family associates.

edges.evidence_quote: Sciascia served as mediator between Bonanno family and Montreal's Rizzuto family in the 1990s.
edges.context: Bonanno capo Gerlando Sciascia mediated between the Bonanno and Rizzuto families.

edges.evidence_quote: he and Dara met with Lucchese crime family capo Paul Vario and acting boss Ettore Coco in Miami in order to keep the peace between the two crime families.
edges.context: Bonanno and Lucchese families met to maintain peace.

edges.evidence_quote: former associate with the Bath Avenue crew, a follower and subgroup of the Bonanno crime family.
edges.context: Bath Avenue crew is a subgroup of the Bonanno family.

edges.evidence_quote: He teamed up with Gambino family boss John Gotti to reinstate the Bonanno family on the Mafia Commission
edges.context: Bonanno boss Massino cooperated with Gambino boss Gotti to reinstate Bonanno on the Commission.

edges.evidence_quote: Pisciotti assaulted several Genovese crime family associates in a Little Italy restaurant.
edges.context: Bonanno acting capo Pisciotti assaulted Genovese associates, leading to conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: In January 2018, Lovaglio testified against Philadelphia crime family boss Joey Merlino and recounted befriending Merlino in 2015.
edges.context: Bonanno soldier Lovaglio befriended Philadelphia boss Merlino, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: the family maintained close ties to the Sicilian Mafia, with whom it orchestrated the importation of billions of dollars' worth of heroin into the United States during the mid-to-late 20th century.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Sicilian Mafia in heroin trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: The New Springville Boys – a 'farm team' for the Bonanno and Colombo families
edges.context: New Springville Boys served as a farm team for both Bonanno and Colombo families.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bonanno family maintained an association with the Rochester crime family.
edges.context: Bonanno family had an association with Rochester crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: some remnants of the Rochester Mafia, including Thomas 'Tommy' Marotta joined the Bonanno family, while others, such as Rene Piccarreto, instead aligned with the Buffalo crime family.
edges.context: Rochester remnants aligned with either Bonanno or Buffalo families, indicating indirect connection.

edges.evidence_quote: Carmine Galante to Montreal ... to supervise the family drug business there, where he worked with the Calabrian Vincenzo Cotroni of the Cotroni family in the French Connection heroin smuggling scheme.
edges.context: Bonanno family cooperated with Cotroni family in heroin smuggling.

edges.evidence_quote: The New Springville Boys – a 'farm team' for the Bonanno and Colombo families
edges.context: New Springville Boys is a farm team for the Bonanno family.

edges.evidence_quote: As tensions grew into a power struggle between the Calabrian and Sicilian factions of the family, a mob war began in 1973.
edges.context: The Cotroni and Rizzuto families were factions within the same organization and engaged in a violent power struggle.

edges.evidence_quote: The FBI considers both the Cotronis and the Rizzutos to be factions of the Bonanno family
edges.context: The FBI classifies the Rizzuto family as a faction of the Bonanno crime family.
